count_state.note: GLD.json covers 2004-11-18 to 2026-07-02 (5438 rows) -- real intraday OHLC from its very first row (no close-only prefix, unlike GSPC/DJI). Own-longest-series self-anchor (dashboard_build_spec.md §2: "GLD, SLV, CL, BTC | own longest series"), independent of any other instrument's degree claim -- gold is its own asset class, no equity/bond "principle of commonality" stand-in applies (unlike DIA/QQQ's original ^GSPC stand-in, dashboard_build_spec.md §18.2). The fixed Intermediate (IV)/(V) anchor intentionally omits a (III)-equivalent peak label for gold's own real 2011 high -- see degree.intermediate.note.

lenses.name: hurst_underlying_trend
lenses.reason: Hickson underlying-trend vote across cycles longer than 80D (20W:down, 40W:down, 18M:up, 54M:up, 9Y:up, 18Y:up): normalized=+0.33 (6 cycle(s) counted)

lenses.reason: ^DJI last low 45057.28 @ 2026-03-30 (down) vs ^DJT last low 19504.20 @ 2026-05-14 (up) -- diverging, medium scale

lenses.reason: Summation Index now 1874.1 vs neutral 1000 (neutral-zone); votes only at the +2000/-1000 thresholds, no decision in between

lenses.name: comparative_spine_slv
lenses.reason: GLD/SLV (SLV = Silver): GLD -7.3% / ratio +11.6% from 2026-06-03 to 2026-07-02 (20 sessions) -- diverging; gold/silver ratio read (same relationship viewed from gold's side: GLD/SLV rising = gold outperforming silver = safe-haven-led/risk-off; falling = silver outperforming = industrial-demand-led/risk-on), never a market-direction vote

lenses.name: comparative_spine_tlt
lenses.reason: GLD/TLT (TLT = 20+ Year Treasury Bonds): GLD -7.3% / ratio -7.5% from 2026-06-03 to 2026-07-02 (20 sessions) -- aligned; real-yield/inflation-expectations read (gold vs. nominal long treasuries), never a market-direction vote

lenses.name: comparative_spine_dxy
lenses.reason: GLD/DXY (DXY = US Dollar Index): GLD -7.3% / ratio -8.5% from 2026-06-03 to 2026-07-02 (20 sessions) -- aligned; cross-asset macro/rate-differential read, never a market-direction vote

invalidation.basis: A confirmed daily close below the real 2015-12-17 low would invalidate the Intermediate (V)/Minor-in-progress hypothesis.

breadth.nh_nl: 163 new highs / 38 new lows (net +125) as of 2026-07-01 -- stockcharts.com $NYHGH/$NYLOW Prev Close [cross-check vs barchart.com net +117, agrees within tolerance (6.4% diff)]

mcclellan.narrative: no named Oscillator pattern currently active

pring_kst.lens_basis: TWO voting lenses: kst_long_term = monthly KST (dominant 24-mo ROC, ~half the ~4yr business cycle), anchored on GLD, vs its 9-month signal MA; kst_short_term = daily KST (dominant 30-day ROC) on GLD itself, vs its 10-day signal MA. Intermediate (weekly) KST is display-only, never votes -- see market_cycle_model.intermediate.
pring_kst.known_failure_mode: false bear signals during secular/linear uptrends (Pring 4th ed. p.241, his own Nikkei 1975-92 example); dominant spans assume the ~4-yr business cycle (long) or can lag a fast whipsaw (short)

trace.hurst: lib.hurst_overlay.build_hurst_overlay(GLD.json, 20D/40D/80D/20W/40W, origin_date=None -> each cycle's own earliest detected trough)
trace.hurst.underlying_trend: lib.hurst_fld.underlying_trend(GLD.json, cycle) per cycle -- Hickson Concept #10 time_translation nested inside; descriptive only (see MCCLELLAN_NARRATIVE_PROTOCOL.md §1)
trace.vtl: lib.vtl.confirmed_turns(GLD.json, scale='medium') -- also feeds the header's 'vtl' directional lens (most recently CONFIRMED higher-degree turn by confirmed_on: trough=up, peak=down, per MotiveWave pp.17-18)
trace.local_wave_counts: lib.ew_count.find_all_impulses(GLD.json, scale) for slow/medium/micro -- rule-validated, NOT the human-reviewed EW count; state='none' per-scale if nothing passes the 3 hard rules
trace.invalidation: counts/GLD.json invalidation (inherited from counts/GLD.json, GLD has none of its own)
trace.comparative_spine: lib.ratio.ratio_series/trend_divergence_flag on GLD vs SLV/TLT/DXY -- divergence flag computed on the FULL common history; the stored ratio series is sliced to the display window (payload only, not a signal change)
trace.dow_theory: lib.dow_theory.confirm_state(DJI.json, DJT.json, scale=medium) -- market-wide, identical on every reference panel
trace.breadth: lib.breadth_calc.enrich(data/breadth/nyse.json) -- market-wide, identical on every reference panel
trace.breadth.nh_nl: fetch_nhnl.py: stockcharts.com $NYHGH/$NYLOW Prev Close (OCR, primary) cross-checked against barchart.com $MAHN/$MALN previousClose (plain JSON) -> data/breadth/nhnl.json, one trading day behind (see that script's docstring); falls back to a 'data pending' string if nhnl.json doesn't exist yet
trace.mcclellan: lib.mcclellan_summation on nyse.json summ_pub + GLD.json -- Summation itself is market-wide, the price target is instrument-specific
trace.mcclellan.patterns: lib.mcclellan_oscillator.classify(nyse.json mosc_pub, mcc_state, pivots_medium, summ_pivots, NEUTRAL) -- 'Patterns for Profit' descriptive classifier (triple top/bottom read on the Summation, not the Oscillator -- corrected after reading the book's own figure, see MCCLELLAN_NARRATIVE_PROTOCOL.md); NOT a backtested signal (TRIALS_LEDGER ID-004/006/009 NULL on related McClellan hypotheses)
trace.scenario_cone: repackaged directly from hurst_fld.forecast + mcclellan_summation forecast entries above -- no separate projection computed
trace.agreement.pivot_independence: distinct pivot SCALES (lib.pivots.SCALES / lib.hurst_fld.NOMINAL) actually feeding the directional lenses above
trace.jenkins.circular_arc: lib.circular_arcs.arc_from_pivots(GLD.json, 'medium') -- Jenkins 3-pivot circumcircle, ATR-normalized; directional lens = center-side structure read (center above all pivots = up-curving support rim = up); turn_date = arc-goes-vertical 3/9-o'clock timing (Geometry Fig 9); no price target by design -- both candidate price readings proved structurally unsound (spec §13)
trace.jenkins.fib_turn_cluster: lib.fib_time_clusters.forecast_turn_cluster(GLD.json, 'medium') -- strongest forward Fibonacci bar-count cluster (>=2 DISTINCT pivots converging); a turn DATE only, deliberately NOT a directional vote in the agreement score (a turn window has no direction; inventing one would fabricate data)
trace.ferrera.composite_state: lib.ferrera_composite.state(build_date) -- Ferrera DTF 16-cycle triangle-wave composite (table locked in print May 2002); directional lens = composite slope sign (net phase vote), omitted while flat; next_turn joins the timing strip at the source's own +/-6mo tolerance; market-wide/calendar-only, identical on every reference panel
trace.ferrera.benner: lib.benner_sequence.next_events(build_date) -- Benner year sequences (tops 8-9-10 from 1972, lows 7-11-9 from 1978, panics 16-18-20 from 1965); YEAR resolution timing markers only, never a directional vote; market-wide/calendar-only
trace.pring_kst: lib.kst.market_cycle_model(GLD.json, long_bars=GLD.json) -- Pring 4th ed. Table 12-1/12-2 (OCR-verified, see lib/kst.py grounding); header lenses 'kst_long_term' (monthly KST on GLD vs its 9-mo MA) and 'kst_short_term' (daily KST on GLD vs its 10-day MA), both state-until-crossed; intermediate (weekly) KST is display context only, never votes; newest point on every variant uses the RUNNING month/week/day close (partial_last_period flag carried), so a lens can move intra-period but history never repaints
